description_en: Classic Ming dynasty yoke-back armchair (四出头官帽椅) with S-curved splat, protruding top rail resembling an official's hat, splayed legs with stretchers, and forward-protruding armrests supported by gooseneck posts. Features minimal ornamentation and elegant proportions typical of scholarly furniture.

components.name: front_left_leg
components.description: Front left support post, square section, splayed outward
components.position_note: front left corner, extends from floor to seat frame underside

components.name: front_right_leg
components.description: Front right support post, square section, splayed outward
components.position_note: front right corner, extends from floor to seat frame underside

components.name: back_left_leg
components.description: Back left post extending full height from floor to top rail, continuous piece
components.position_note: back left corner, extends from floor to support top rail

components.name: back_right_leg
components.description: Back right post extending full height from floor to top rail, continuous piece
components.position_note: back right corner, extends from floor to support top rail

components.name: front_seat_rail
components.description: Front horizontal frame member of the seat
components.position_note: front edge between front legs, at 0.52m height

components.name: back_seat_rail
components.description: Back horizontal frame member of the seat
components.position_note: back edge between back legs, at 0.52m height

components.name: side_seat_rail
components.description: Side horizontal frame members connecting front and back rails
components.position_note: left and right edges between front and back rails

components.name: seat_panel
components.description: Wooden seat board set within the frame
components.position_note: inset within the four seat rails, forming sitting surface

components.name: back_splat
components.description: Central S-curved backrest panel (靠背板) with cyma recta profile, vertical between seat and top rail
components.position_note: centered between back legs in the frontal plane, vertical S-curve panel connecting the center of back seat rail to the center of top rail between the back legs

components.name: top_rail
components.description: Curved yoke-shaped top rail (搭脑) extending beyond back posts
components.position_note: across top of back legs, ends protrude backward like hat wings

components.name: arm_support
components.description: Curved gooseneck post (鹅脖) splayed outward, supporting front of armrest
components.position_note: front of each armrest, rising from front leg at 0.52m to armrest at 0.72m

components.name: armrest
components.description: Horizontal armrest rail (扶手) extending from back post over gooseneck, protruding forward
components.position_note: left and right sides, from back leg at 0.72m height forward over gooseneck, end protrudes beyond support

components.name: front_apron
components.description: Curved apron board under front seat rail
components.position_note: between front seat rail and front legs, beaded edge, front face flush with leg front faces

components.name: side_apron
components.description: Curved apron boards under side seat rails
components.position_note: left and right sides between side seat rails and legs, outer face flush with leg outer faces

components.name: front_stretcher
components.position_note: between front legs, lowest position (~0.15m from floor)

components.name: back_stretcher
components.description: Back lower stretcher connecting back legs
components.position_note: between back legs, highest position (~0.25m from floor)

components.name: side_stretcher
components.description: Side stretchers connecting front and back legs
components.position_note: left and right sides, middle height (~0.20m from floor)

style_notes: Ming dynasty scholarly elegance with 'four protrusions' (四出头) where top rail ends extend backward and armrest ends extend forward beyond posts. Legs exhibit '侧脚' (splay) for stability. Back splat features classic vertical S-curve (cyma recta) profile for ergonomic support. Armrests supported by curved gooseneck posts (鹅脖) splayed outward. Stretchers follow '步步高' (step-by-step rise) pattern: front lowest, sides middle, back highest. Simple beaded edges on aprons; minimal carving to emphasize wood grain and joinery. Wood: dense hardwood like huanghuali or walnut, warm amber-brown tone.
